pageSubtitle: v5.1 Per-Sector Calibrated Model — 16 features, temporal validation, PU-learning

overview.description: RUBLI v5.1 scores every contract with a statistical risk indicator using per-sector logistic regression trained on 22 documented cases. Scores measure similarity to known corruption patterns — the model was trained on contracts through 2020 and tested on 2021+.

riskLevels.title: Risk Levels
riskLevels.disclaimer: Risk scores are statistical indicators with 95% confidence intervals. A high score indicates similarity to documented corruption patterns — it does not constitute proof.

coefficients.title: v5.1 Global Model Coefficients
coefficients.subtitle: 16 z-score features ranked by absolute coefficient magnitude. Positive = increases risk; negative = decreases risk.

keyFindings.title: What the Model Learned
keyFindings.concentrationDetail: Vendor concentration, price volatility, and win rate are the top 3 predictors. These reflect Mexico's documented corruption pattern: large vendors capturing institutional monopolies.
keyFindings.directAwardDetail: In v4.0, the direct_award coefficient was -0.197 (counterintuitive). With diversified ground truth and new features in v5.1, it correctly flipped to +0.182.
keyFindings.singleBidWeak: Single bidding is a weak signal
keyFindings.singleBidDetail: Despite being the #1 indicator in European datasets, single bidding has a near-zero coefficient in Mexico. This reflects the 70% direct award rate — competitive procedures are rare, so single bids carry less information.
keyFindings.coBiddingZeroDetail: Regularized to exactly 0.000 in all models. The training cases involve concentration, not collusion rings.

validation.title: Ground Truth Validation
validation.description: Detection rates on 22 documented corruption cases (26,704 contracts from 27 matched vendors).
validation.footnote: * Case 9 shares vendors with Case 5 (Odebrecht). Documented for reference.

statisticalMethods.zScoreDesc: Each feature is standardized relative to its sector and year baseline. A direct award in Defensa (80% are direct) is less suspicious than in Educación (50% are direct). Z-scores capture this context.
statisticalMethods.mahalanobisDesc: Measures how unusual a contract's feature vector is in multidimensional space. Replaces the 5 hardcoded interaction pairs from v3.3 with a full covariance matrix that captures all pairwise relationships.
statisticalMethods.perSectorDesc: v5.1 trains 12 dedicated models (one per sector) plus a global fallback. Corruption patterns differ by sector — Energía is driven by industry mismatch, while Agricultura is driven by vendor concentration (+1.82).
statisticalMethods.perSectorFormula: S(z) = σ(β₀ + βᵀz) / c, where c = 0.882 (PU correction)

v33.title: Previous Model: v3.3 (Weighted Checklist)
v33.description: v3.3 used 8 expert-weighted factors based on IMF CRI methodology. Preserved in risk_score_v3 column for comparison.

dataSources.title: Data Sources — COMPRANET
dataSources.description: Data quality varies significantly by period. Risk scores for 2002–2010 are directional estimates with lower reliability.

pageSubline: Statistical analysis of 3.1M Mexican government procurement contracts

groundTruth.pageSubtitle: Documented corruption cases validate the risk detection model
groundTruth.callout: This page measures RUBLI's detection capability against real, documented Mexican procurement corruption cases. Each case was independently investigated by journalists, auditors, or prosecutors. Our model never saw these labels during training — the detection rates shown here represent genuine out-of-sample predictive performance.
groundTruth.liveDetectionRates: Live detection rates computed from current risk scores. Click a row to expand.
groundTruth.noCasesFound: No ground truth cases found in the database.
groundTruth.failedSummary: Failed to load summary data. Please try refreshing.
groundTruth.factorLiftDesc: How much more often each risk factor appears in documented corruption cases vs the general contract population. Lift > 1.0 = over-represented in known-bad contracts.
groundTruth.modelComparisonDesc: Detection rate comparison across model versions (v3.3, v4.0, v5.1). High+ = score >= 0.30, Critical = score >= 0.50.
groundTruth.noModelComparison: No model comparison data available. Run validate_risk_model.py to generate results.

modelTransparency.disclaimer: Risk scores are statistical indicators, not legal conclusions. High scores indicate similarity to documented corruption patterns — they do not constitute proof.
modelTransparency.subtitleLive: Understanding how the {{version}} risk scoring model works — coefficients, validation, and limitations
modelTransparency.bannerLive: Coefficient chart and model comparison are loaded from the live API. Use the sector selector to view per-sector model weights.
modelTransparency.bannerFallback: Falling back to static documentation data. Live endpoints will be used when available.

coeffChart.title: What Drives Risk Scores?
coeffChart.description: Logistic regression coefficients — larger bars mean stronger influence on the risk score
coeffChart.callout: Each coefficient represents the change in log-odds of corruption per 1 standard deviation increase in the z-scored feature. Positive values increase risk; negative values decrease it.
coeffChart.calloutCI: Error bars show 95% bootstrap confidence intervals (1,000 resamples).

perSector.title: Per-Sector Models
perSector.description: Top 3 factors for each of the 12 per-sector logistic regression sub-models
perSector.fallbackNote: Sector models are applied when the contract's sector has 30+ contracts in the training data. Global model is used as fallback.

comparison.title: Model Comparison: v3.3 vs v5.1
comparison.description: Per-sector framework (v5.1) dramatically outperforms the weighted checklist (v3.3) on every metric

perCase.title: Per-Case Detection Performance
perCase.description: Detection performance on 22 documented corruption cases — 15 have matched vendor contracts, 7 pending or zero matches
perCase.callout: Detection % measures the share of contracts from each case that the model flags at medium risk or above (score >= {{medium}}). High+ % measures the share flagged at high or critical levels (score >= {{high}}). Cases with more contracts provide stronger validation signal.

limitations.warning: No model is perfect. These limitations are disclosed for transparency. A high risk score indicates statistical anomaly consistent with corruption patterns — it does not constitute proof of wrongdoing.
limitations.groundTruthConcDesc: v5.1 includes 22 documented cases across all 12 sectors (including SAT EFOS ghost companies). However, three cases (IMSS, Segalmex, COVID-19) still account for the majority of training contracts. Vendor concentration remains dominant across most per-sector sub-models.
limitations.smallCaseDesc: Cases with few contracts have lower detection rates: La Estafa Maestra (10 contracts, 0% high+), Oceanografia (2 contracts, 50% detection). The model requires sufficient contract volume to detect patterns reliably.
limitations.noCausalDesc: A high P(corrupt|z) indicates a statistical anomaly consistent with corruption patterns observed in 22 documented cases. It does not prove corruption. Some sectors (defense, energy) have structural reasons for high vendor concentration that are legitimate.
limitations.dataQualityDesc: Structure A data (2002-2010) has only 0.1% RFC coverage, making vendor identification unreliable for that period. Z-scores and risk scores for earlier contracts may be less accurate. Network analysis is most reliable for 2010+ data where RFC coverage exceeds 15%.

pipeline.title: How the Score is Computed
pipeline.description: End-to-end pipeline from raw contract data to risk score
pipeline.step1Desc: Each of the 16 features is normalized relative to its sector and year baseline, producing z-scores that measure how unusual a contract is compared to peers.
pipeline.step2Desc: The z-score vector is evaluated against the sector covariance matrix (Ledoit-Wolf shrinkage) to detect multivariate anomalies — unusual combinations of factors.
pipeline.step3Desc: Cross-validated ElasticNet (C=10.0, l1_ratio=0.25) with 12 per-sector sub-models trained on known-bad contracts from 22 corruption cases converts z-scores into risk scores.
pipeline.step4Desc: Elkan & Noto (2008) holdout PU-learning correction (c=0.8815) adjusts for unlabeled corrupt contracts. Bootstrap (500 resamples) provides 95% confidence intervals.
